era: Hermosa Beach / Los Angeles / touring, 1976-1986. Hardcore. E=9 -- TIES S36 ABSOLUTE MAXIMUM. Seattle influence entry.

location.influence_circuit: Seattle, Washington -- Metropolis venue, West Coast touring axis, SST distribution network

seattle_influence_note: NOT a Seattle band. Hermosa Beach / LA. This entry documents their direct influence on Seattle's hardcore scene via: (1) SST distribution network reaching Pacific Northwest stores, (2) touring circuit through Seattle's Metropolis venue, (3) direct influence on The Fartz (degree 270) and other early Seattle hardcore bands.

theory_nodes.concept: SST pipeline to Seattle: Greg Ginn founded SST (1978) to release Black Flag. SST distribution network reached Pacific Northwest record stores. A teenager in Seattle in 1982 could buy Damaged, My War, and Nervous Breakdown at a local shop.
theory_nodes.description: SST Records as the mechanism. Label degree 261, band degree 287.

theory_nodes.concept: E=9 ties new S36 absolute maximum. Second consecutive E=9 (286 Dead Kennedys, 287 Black Flag). Engine has never sustained maximum energy across consecutive degrees. Hardcore block 285-288 energy arc: 7-9-9-9.
theory_nodes.description: Consecutive ceiling: the 286-287 pair is the most intense consecutive pairing in the engine.

theory_nodes.concept: My War side B (1984): three long, slow, crushing songs anticipating sludge metal. Band refused to remain frozen in the form it created. The evolution beyond hardcore is a specific contribution to rock history.
theory_nodes.description: My War as the evolution document: hardcore to proto-sludge.

theory_nodes.concept: The touring ethic: Black Flag treated touring as full-time occupation. West Coast corridor Los Angeles-SF-Portland-Seattle. Played Metropolis Seattle. Black Flag live was a physical ordeal. Henry Rollins's body as musical instrument.
theory_nodes.description: Direct transfer via live performance: the Metropolis shows.

theory_nodes.concept: SST/AT mirror at degrees 261/270/286/287: Alternative Tentacles (AT, San Francisco, Jello Biafra) and SST Records (LA, Greg Ginn) were both poles of West Coast hardcore distribution. Both reached Seattle. 286-287 pair documents both poles simultaneously.
theory_nodes.description: The West Coast hardcore infrastructure mirror.

cross_references.relationship: Black Flag's label. Founded by Greg Ginn 1978. The distribution infrastructure. Engine entry at degree 261 documented the label; degree 287 documents the band that forced the label into existence.
Seattle hardcore, Alternative Tentacles. The primary Seattle output of the LA hardcore input. Fartz formed in dialogue with the template Black Flag established.
FIRST E=9. Alternative Tentacles, SF. Consecutive E=9 pair with Black Flag. SF band (DK) + LA band (BF) at ceiling in consecutive degrees. Both documented as Seattle influence entries.
Third consecutive E=9. Post-hardcore, Shoreline. The hardcore DNA absorbed, mutated, and continued. E=9 holds for three straight degrees.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 + E=7 = E=16. Both are ensemble phenomena at maximum intensity: Black Flag's collective assault, the colony's overlapping guttural croaking roar. Different scales, same principle -- collective noise as primary force.
